perturbation.original_text: Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient shall, within twenty (20) calendar days, either: (i) return the Confidential Materials in its possession to Premisys and instruct its Representatives to do the same; (ii) destroy the Confidential Materials in a secure manner and instruct its Representatives to do the same; or (iii) at Premisys' election, take any combination of steps (i) and (ii). Prior to the expiration of the foregoing twenty (20) period, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5 (a "COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE"). Notwithstanding the -2- <PAGE> provisions of this Section 5, if Recipient receives a Termination Notice, it shall not be required to return or destroy Confidential Materials to the extent that, in the reasonable opinion of legal counsel for such party, such return or destruction violates any law or regulation; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that in such event Recipient shall describe in its Compliance Certificate the extent to which it is not returning or destroying Confidential Materials. Notwithstanding any return or destruction of Confidential Materials, Recipient will continue to be bound by its obligations under this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information contained or reflected in such Confidential Materials.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient shall, within a reasonable period of time, either: (i) return the Confidential Materials in its possession to Premisys and instruct its Representatives to do the same; (ii) destroy the Confidential Materials and instruct its Representatives to do the same; or (iii) at Premisys' election, take any combination of steps (i) and (ii). Prior to completing the above steps, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5 (a "COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE"). Notwithstanding the -2- <PAGE> provisions of this Section 5, if Recipient receives a Termination Notice, it shall not be required to return or destroy Confidential Materials. Notwithstanding any return or destruction of Confidential Materials, Recipient will continue to be bound by its obligations under this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information contained or reflected in such Confidential Materials.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces ambiguity by changing 'twenty (20) calendar days' to 'a reasonable period of time'. It also removes the clause that specifies that a Recipient doesn't have to return or destroy Confidential Materials to the extent that, in the reasonable opinion of legal counsel for such party, such return or destruction violates any law or regulation. The modification also removes the requirement of the Recipient describing in its Compliance Certificate the extent to which it is not returning or destroying Confidential Materials. It also changes 'Prior to the expiration of the foregoing twenty (20) period, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5' to 'Prior to completing the above steps, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5', making it ambiguous on when it should be certified. This contradicts the clear timeline and obligations outlined in the original Section 5, potentially leading to disputes over compliance and enforcement. The original version provides a definitive timeframe of 20 days for compliance. The modified version replaces this with 'reasonable time', which lacks a specific measure, creating uncertainty. This conflicts with the purpose of section 5.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RETURN/DESTRUCTION OF CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. Premisys may, at any time, deliver written notice (a "TERMINATION NOTICE") to Recipient of Premisys' election to have all tangible materials (including without limitation paper and magnetic storage media) in the possession of Recipient (or its Representatives) which contain, reflect, are based upon or derived from, in whole or in part, any of Premisys' Confidential Information ("CONFIDENTIAL MATERIALS") either returned to Premisys or, at Premisys' election, destroyed. Upon receipt of a Termination Notice, Recipient shall, within a reasonable period of time, either: (i) return the Confidential Materials in its possession to Premisys and instruct its Representatives to do the same; (ii) destroy the Confidential Materials and instruct its Representatives to do the same; or (iii) at Premisys' election, take any combination of steps (i) and (ii). Prior to completing the above steps, Recipient shall certify in writing to Premisys that it has complied with its obligations under this Section 5 (a "COMPLIANCE CERTIFICATE"). Notwithstanding the -2- <PAGE> provisions of this Section 5, if Recipient receives a Termination Notice, it shall not be required to return or destroy Confidential Materials. Notwithstanding any return or destruction of Confidential Materials, Recipient will continue to be bound by its obligations under this Agreement with respect to Confidential Information contained or reflected in such Confidential Materials.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a specific timeline of 'twenty (20) calendar days' with the vague term 'a reasonable period of time'. This introduces ambiguity and contradicts the need for a clear and defined timeline for the return or destruction of confidential materials, which undermines the enforceability of the agreement. Furthermore, it removes the clause where a Recipient doesn't have to return or destroy Confidential Materials to the extent that, in the reasonable opinion of legal counsel for such party, such return or destruction violates any law or regulation, and removes the Recipient's requirement to describe in its Compliance Certificate the extent to which it is not returning or destroying Confidential Materials. This contradicts the original intention and adds ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient acknowledges and agrees that Premisys makes no representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient in connection with the Business Purpose. Recipient agrees that neither Premisys nor its agents, shall have any liability to Recipient or its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of the Confidential Information or any errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations and warranties which are made in the final definitive agreement between the parties hereto regarding any transactions contemplated hereby, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient acknowledges and agrees that Premisys makes no representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy of the Confidential Information disclosed to Recipient in connection with the Business Purpose. Recipient agrees that Premisys nor its agents, shall have any liability to Recipient or its Representatives relating to or resulting from the use of the Confidential Information or any errors therein or omissions therefrom. Only those representations and warranties which are made in the final definitive agreement between the parties hereto regarding any transactions contemplated hereby, when, as and if executed, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.explanation: This removes 'or completeness' from the statement that Premisys makes no representation or warranty to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information, and also introduces a grammatical error by changing 'neither Premisys nor its agents' to 'Premisys nor its agents'. Section 1 defines "Confidential Information" as all information disclosed by Premisys, including documents prepared by the Recipient based on Premisys' information. By removing the disclaimer of warranty for 'completeness,' this change suggests that Premisys *does* warrant that the information it provides is complete. This contradicts the nature of Section 1, because Section 1 specifies that documents prepared by the Recipient are still considered "Confidential Information", meaning that there are instances where information is being supplemented by the Recipient to be considered "Confidential Information", and Premisys cannot guarantee that those documents will be considered complete since they are being created by the Recipient.
perturbation.contradicted_text: DEFINITION OF "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION. As used in this Agreement, "CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION" means all information disclosed by Premisys or its agents to Recipient (or to Recipient's attorneys, accountants or other professional advisors, collectively, its "REPRESENTATIVES") in connection with the Business Purpose, including without limitation, information regarding Premisys' products, computer software, technology, agreements, customers, suppliers, financial condition, business plans or strategies and also includes all information contained in any notes, analyses, compilations, studies, interpretations or other documents prepared by Recipient or its Representatives which contain, reflect or are based upon, in whole or in part, the information furnished by Premisys or its agents; PROVIDED, HOWEVER, that Confidential Information will NOT include information that: (a) is as of the Effective Date, or hereafter becomes, through no act or failure to act on the part of Recipient, generally known or readily ascertainable through proper means to persons knowledgeable in the relevant industry; (b) was acquired by Recipient by proper means without restriction as to use or disclosure BEFORE receiving such information from Premisys; (c) is hereafter rightfully furnished to Recipient by a third party, without restriction as to use or disclosure; or (d) was independently developed by Recipient without use of Premisys' Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes 'or completeness' from the disclaimer of warranty related to Confidential Information. The original text disclaimed both accuracy and completeness. The contradicted text (DEFINITION OF 'CONFIDENTIAL INFORMATION') defines Confidential Information broadly, including documents prepared by the recipient that are based on Premisys's information. Because these documents prepared by the recipient are considered Confidential Information, Premisys cannot guarantee their completeness, as the recipient supplements them. By removing the 'completeness' disclaimer, the changed text implies Premisys *does* warrant the completeness of Confidential Information, which now directly contradicts the definition because the definition includes things that Premisys cannot guarantee are complete.

perturbation.original_text: Beginning on Effective Date and continuing for a period of six months thereafter, Recipient will not, either for itself or for any other person or entity, directly or indirectly, solicit, induce or attempt to induce any employee of Premisys to terminate his or her employment with Premisys. The parties agree that general solicitation or recruitment of prospective employees through newspaper advertising or job fairs shall not be deemed a violation of this provision.
perturbation.changed_text: Beginning on Effective Date and continuing thereafter, Recipient will not, either for itself or for any other person or entity, directly or indirectly, solicit, induce or attempt to induce any employee of Premisys to terminate his or her employment with Premisys. The parties agree that general solicitation or recruitment of prospective employees through newspaper advertising or job fairs shall not be deemed a violation of this provision.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Beginning on Effective Date and continuing for a period of six months thereafter' to 'Beginning on Effective Date and continuing thereafter,' the non-solicitation clause now has no end date. This creates an in-text contradiction with Section 8 which states: 'Each party acknowledges that (i) neither party will be under any obligation of any kind by virtue of this Agreement to enter into any agreement relating to the Business Purpose or the possible transaction referred to herein'. Because there is no end date to the non-solicitation clause, this goes against the acknowledgment that no party should be under obligation of any kind by virtue of this agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party acknowledges that (i) neither party will be under any obligation of any kind by virtue of this Agreement to enter into any agreement relating to the Business Purpose or the possible transaction referred to herein and (ii) unless and until a binding written agreement to the contrary is executed and delivered by the parties hereto, each party reserves the absolute right, in its sole discretion, to reject any and all proposals made by the other party with regard to any possible transaction relating to the Business Purpose and to terminate discussions relating to such a possible transaction at any time.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the non-solicitation clause to six months. The changed text removes this limit, making the non-solicitation clause indefinite. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that neither party will be under any obligation by virtue of the agreement. An indefinite non-solicitation clause constitutes an obligation, creating a clear and strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of litigation arising from or relating to this Agreement, the party which is found to be the prevailing party by a court of competent jurisdiction in a final, non-appealable order shall receive from the non-prevailing party the reasonable legal fees and expenses, including attorney fees, incurred by the prevailing party in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of litigation arising from or relating to this Agreement, the party which is found to be the prevailing party by a court of competent jurisdiction shall receive from the non-prevailing party all the legal fees and expenses, including attorney fees, incurred by the prevailing party in connection with such litigation, including any appeal therefrom.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'in a final, non-appealable order', this now contradicts section 12 of the agreement. Removing the clause creates a contradiction because section 12 limits the Venue to United States District Court for the Northern District of California, which inherently opens the chance for the case to be appealed. Since Section 11 indicates that the winning party will receive all legal fees and expenses, but also removes the the clause 'in a final, non-appealable order', there is now an in-text contradiction that occurs due to this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: GOVERNING LAW; JURISDICTION AND VENUE. The internal laws of the State of California (irrespective of its choice of law principles) will govern the validity of this Agreement, the construction of its terms, and the interpretation and enforcement of the rights and duties of the parties hereto. Each of the parties hereby irrevocably consents to the exclusive jurisdiction of and venue in the United States District Court for the Northern District of California in connection with any litigation of a dispute between them arising from or relating to this Agreement and waives any and all right to object to the jurisdiction of such court or to claim that venue in such court is not proper.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the phrase 'in a final, non-appealable order' from the prevailing party clause, which directly contradicts the governing law section that specifies the venue as the United States District Court for the Northern District of California. Because cases in the US District Court can be appealed, the removal of the 'final, non-appealable order' clause in the Changed Text creates a contradiction with the Contradicted Text (Governing Law; Jurisdiction and Venue), because the original clause in the original text prevents an appeal from opening up the possibility for the non-prevailing party to continuously pay legal fees, which is an in-text contradiction. This creates ambiguity and inconsistency regarding when the prevailing party is entitled to legal fees and expenses.
